role: This file is the complete visual stylesheet for the Right-Hand-Man (RHM) persistent overlay, covering the V handle, fan menu, scrim, greeting, brain panel, note composer, and post composer surfaces.
capabilities: Style persistent draggable V handle with hover/active/greet states
Render open-fan verb arc with live and coming-soon variants
Render scrim veil behind the open fan
Render first-contact greeting card with tail pointer
Render brain panel with starter chips, guided tour stepper, reply area, and brain input
Render note composer panel with aim, meaning, unavailable message, input, status, and save
Render L4 post composer toggle/body/channel/message/send inside brain panel
Apply paper design tokens (no hardcoded colors)
substance_why: The file contains extensive, detailed CSS for every RHM surface—handle, fan, scrim, greeting, brain panel, note composer, and post composer—with states, animations, accessibility focus rings, and token usage.
interactions_present: Hover/active/focus-visible states on handle, verbs, buttons, and inputs
Cursor grab/grabbing on the draggable handle
Open/close affordances styled (close buttons, dismiss/tour buttons, send/save buttons)
interactions_missing: No actual drag behavior or transform updates (only touch-action: none and cursor)
No JavaScript wiring for toggling open/greet states
No fan verb selection or arc positioning logic
No brain starter/tour step progression logic
No note save/send or post send behavior
No scrim click-to-dismiss or escape handling
performance_risks: Multiple fixed-position overlays with high z-index stacking may cause compositing overhead
Animations on opacity/transform are generally safe but fan items use fixed-width cards with box-shadows
Filter drop-shadow on greeting tail adds per-element GPU cost
Overflow:auto panels with max-height and pre-wrapped content can cause layout on large replies
Width expressions using min() with vw may trigger relayout on viewport resize
reuse_verdict_why: The component structure, token-based styling, and interaction states are worth preserving, but the monolithic CSS should be rebuilt alongside the redesigned component architecture.
decomposition_hints: Split handle + fan + scrim into one module
Split greeting card into its own module
Split brain panel into submodules: starters, tour, reply, brain input, post composer
Split note composer into its own module
Extract shared animation keyframes and token variables into a common RHM theme/animation file
